message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracking state

Update .beads/issues.jsonl with latest issue state changes from ongoing
development work. This keeps the local bead database synchronized with
actual progress made across the project.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1gbt5","title":"Add ntm assign --reassign for moving bead between agents","description":"## Overview
Allow users to move an assignment from one agent to another without losing tracking state.

## Use Cases
1. Agent is stuck and user wants to give bead to a faster agent
2. User realizes a bead is better suited for a different agent type
3. Load balancing - redistribute work mid-session

## Usage
```bash
# Reassign bead to a different pane
ntm assign myproject --reassign bd-xyz --to-pane=4

# Reassign to specific agent type (picks idle agent of that type)
ntm assign myproject --reassign bd-xyz --to-type=codex

# Reassign with new prompt
ntm assign myproject --reassign bd-xyz --to-pane=4 --prompt="Continue from where the previous agent left off"
```

## Workflow
1. Validate bead is currently assigned
2. Get current assignment info
3. Update assignment status to "reassigned"
4. Create new assignment for target agent
5. Release file reservations from old agent (if any)
6. Create file reservations for new agent (if any)
7. Send prompt to new agent
8. Return reassignment result

## Output (Standard Envelope)
```json
{
  "command": "assign",
  "subcommand": "reassign",
  "session": "myproject",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-19T12:00:00Z",
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "reassigned": {
      "bead_id": "bd-xyz",
      "bead_title": "Implement feature X",
      "pane": 4,
      "agent_type": "codex",
      "agent_name": "BlueLake",
      "status": "assigned",
      "prompt_sent": true,
      "assigned_at": "2026-01-19T12:00:00Z",
      "previous_pane": 2,
      "previous_agent": "GreenCastle",
      "previous_agent_type": "claude",
      "previous_status": "working",
      "file_reservations_transferred": true
    }
  },
  "warnings": [],
  "error": null
}
```

## Error Output
```json
{
  "command": "assign",
  "subcommand": "reassign",
  "session": "myproject",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-19T12:00:00Z",
  "success": false,
  "data": null,
  "warnings": [],
  "error": {
    "code": "TARGET_BUSY",
    "message": "pane 4 already has assignment bd-abc",
    "details": {
      "current_bead": "bd-abc",
      "current_status": "working"
    }
  }
}
```

## Error Codes
- NOT_ASSIGNED: Bead doesn't have an active assignment
- TARGET_BUSY: Target pane already has an assignment
- PANE_NOT_FOUND: Target pane doesn't exist
- NO_IDLE_AGENT: No idle agent of specified type

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Reassign bead to specific pane
- [ ] Reassign by agent type (auto-select idle)
- [ ] Transfer file reservations via Agent Mail
- [ ] Update assignment store correctly
- [ ] Optional custom prompt
- [ ] Standard JSON envelope output
- [ ] Clear error codes

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Assignment state update logic
- Unit test: File reservation transfer
- Unit test: Output format validation
- E2E test: Full reassignment workflow","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:36:52.859683490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T03:55:18.854201422Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T03:55:18.854147310Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gbt5","depends_on_id":"bd-1ohy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:38:38.231148699Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gbt5","depends_on_id":"bd-92e1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:38:24.736944643Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gbt5","depends_on_id":"bd-k3zq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:38:31.515777835Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

+{"id":"bd-1gclg","title":"Add comprehensive --label integration test script","description":"## Context
The --label feature (GH#49) was implemented across spawn, create, quick, add, and robot commands
but lacks end-to-end integration testing. The existing unit tests in label_test.go cover parsing,
formatting, validation, and GetProjectDir, but there is no test that exercises the full workflow:
creating labeled sessions, verifying they share a project directory, adding agents to them, and
listing them.

## Scope
Create a comprehensive integration test file: internal/cli/label_integration_test.go

### Test cases to cover:
1. **spawn --label creates correctly named session**
   - ntm spawn testproj --label frontend --cc=1 --dry-run
   - Verify session name = "testproj--frontend"
   - Verify project dir = projectsBase/testproj (NOT projectsBase/testproj--frontend)

2. **Multiple labels share same project directory**
   - create testproj--frontend and testproj--backend
   - Both resolve to same GetProjectDir()

3. **create --label works**
   - ntm create testproj --label staging
   - Session name = "testproj--staging"

4. **quick --label works**
   - ntm quick testproj --label dev
   - Session name = "testproj--dev"

5. **add --label targets correct session**
   - ntm add testproj --label frontend --cc=1
   - Targets session "testproj--frontend"

6. **Validation rejects invalid labels**
   - --label "bad!" (special chars)
   - --label "my--label" (contains separator)
   - --label (51+ chars) (too long)

7. **Validation rejects project names with -- (unconditional for spawn/create/quick)**
   - ntm spawn my--project → error
   - ntm spawn my--project --label x → error

8. **Robot mode --spawn-label**
   - ntm --robot-spawn=testproj --spawn-label=frontend --spawn-cc=1 --dry-run
   - Verify JSON output contains correct session name

9. **REST API label field**
   - POST /api/v1/sessions/testproj/agents/spawn with {"label":"frontend"}
   - Verify response

### E2E test script: scripts/test-label-e2e.sh
Bash script that exercises the full label workflow with real tmux sessions (requires tmux).
Should clean up after itself.

## Notes
- Use --dry-run where possible to avoid requiring tmux for unit tests
- The integration tests should use table-driven patterns matching existing ntm test style
- Include detailed logging so failures are diagnosable

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] internal/cli/label_integration_test.go exists with all 9 test case groups
- [ ] scripts/test-label-e2e.sh exists and passes on a system with tmux installed
- [ ] All 9 test case groups pass: go test ./internal/cli/ -run TestLabel -v
- [ ] E2E script produces clear PASS/FAIL output with timestamps for each step
- [ ] E2E script cleans up all tmux sessions it creates (even on failure)
- [ ] Tests cover both happy paths and validation error paths
- [ ] No flaky tests (no timing dependencies except E2E script which uses explicit waits)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:28.630358457Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:39.854853655Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gclg","depends_on_id":"bd-2e97t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:32.824927513Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1ghcp","title":"RCH: Implement rch binary detection and worker availability check","description":"Create utility function to detect RCH and check worker status.

## Requirements
1. Check if 'rch' binary exists on PATH
2. Verify rch version is compatible
3. Check if any workers are configured and available
4. Cache result with short TTL (workers may come/go)

## Implementation Location
Create new file: internal/integrations/rch/detect.go

```go
package rch

import (
    "encoding/json"
    "os/exec"
    "sync"
    "time"
)

type RCHStatus struct {
    Available    bool
    Version      string
    WorkerCount  int
    HealthyCount int
    LastCheck    time.Time
}

var (
    mu     sync.RWMutex
    status RCHStatus
    ttl    = 30 * time.Second
)

func GetStatus() RCHStatus {
    mu.RLock()
    if time.Since(status.LastCheck) < ttl {
        defer mu.RUnlock()
        return status
    }
    mu.RUnlock()
    
    return refresh()
}

func refresh() RCHStatus {
    mu.Lock()
    defer mu.Unlock()
    
    // Check binary
    // Run 'rch status --json'
    // Parse worker info
    status.LastCheck = time.Now()
    return status
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:07:32.155904396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:07:34.350046341Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:07:34.349997629Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete: internal/tools/rch.go with RCHAdapter following the DCG adapter pattern. Includes binary detection, version checking, worker availability with 30s TTL caching. Tests in internal/tools/rch_test.go all pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ghcp","depends_on_id":"bd-1c9ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:07:32.180157994Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1gkal","title":"Implement bulk prompt delivery","description":"Implement bulk prompt delivery.

## Functionality

Send assignment prompts to multiple panes efficiently.

## Default Prompt Template

```
Read AGENTS.md, register with Agent Mail. Work on: {bead_id} - {bead_title}.
Use br show {bead_id} for details. Mark in_progress when starting. Use ultrathink.
```

## Template Variables

- {bead_id}: The bead ID
- {bead_title}: Bead title/description
- {bead_type}: task, feature, bug, etc.
- {bead_deps}: Dependencies list
- {session}: Session name
- {pane}: Pane number

## Delivery Strategy

Option A: Sequential (safer)
- Send to pane 2, wait 1s
- Send to pane 3, wait 1s
- etc.

Option B: Parallel (faster)
- Send to all panes simultaneously
- May overwhelm if too many

Recommend: Sequential by default, --parallel flag for advanced users.

## Custom Templates

--prompt-template=<FILE> for custom prompts:
```
Custom marching orders here.
Bead: {bead_id}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:31:28.015184270Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T02:09:42.752527055Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T02:09:42.751110447Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gkal","depends_on_id":"bd-t9qp4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:31:28.043619780Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Unit tests for ntm assign --reassign functionality.

- Reassign completed bead (should error)
- Reassign failed bead (should succeed)

## Logging
- Log reassignment decisions
- Log file reservation transfer
- Log errors with context

## Test File

 {"id":"bd-2dyqk","title":"caut: Implement background polling for usage data","description":"Create background goroutine that polls caut for usage data.

## Requirements

1. Poll caut at configured interval (default 60s)
2. Parse JSON output into internal structs
3. Update in-memory cache for dashboard
4. Trigger alerts when thresholds exceeded

## Implementation

```go
package caut

type UsagePoller struct {
    config   *config.CautConfig
    cache    *UsageCache
    interval time.Duration
    stopCh   chan struct{}
    alertCh  chan Alert
}

func (p *UsagePoller) Start() {
    go p.pollLoop()
}

func (p *UsagePoller) pollLoop() {
    ticker := time.NewTicker(p.interval)
    defer ticker.Stop()
    
    for {
        select {
        case <-ticker.C:
            usage, err := p.fetchUsage()
            if err != nil {
                // Log error, continue
                continue
            }
            p.cache.Update(usage)
            p.checkAlerts(usage)
        case <-p.stopCh:
            return
        }
    }
}

func (p *UsagePoller) fetchUsage() (*Usage, error) {
    cmd := exec.Command("caut", "status", "--json")
    // Parse output
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/integrations/caut/poller.go
- internal/integrations/caut/cache.go

## Testing
- Mock caut output, verify parsing
- Verify cache updates
- Verify alert triggering","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:59:05.756603440Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T16:50:02.306935382Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T16:50:02.306888674Z","close_reason":"Implemented background polling with UsagePoller and UsageCache in internal/integrations/caut/","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2dyqk","depends_on_id":"bd-2lsyy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:59:05.861524087Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2dyqk","depends_on_id":"bd-c749y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:00:14.889118196Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2dyqk","depends_on_id":"bd-d9jwi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:00:21.365802559Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

+{"id":"bd-2e97t","title":"Fix ValidateProjectName inconsistency in robot spawn path","description":"## Context
During the --label feature implementation (GH#49), ValidateProjectName() was added to reject
project names containing '--' (the reserved label separator). However, the validation is applied
INCONSISTENTLY between CLI and robot spawn paths:

### CLI spawn.go (CORRECT — unconditional):
```go
if err := config.ValidateProjectName(sessionName); err != nil {
    return err
}
if label != "" { ... }
```

### Robot spawn.go (BUG — conditional):
```go
if opts.Label != "" {
    if err := config.ValidateProjectName(opts.Session); err != nil { ... }
    ...
}
```

## Impact
The robot spawn path (used by --robot-spawn flag and REST API POST /api/v1/sessions/{id}/agents/spawn)
allows creating sessions with '--' in the project name when no label is specified. This creates
ambiguous session names that break ParseSessionLabel() round-trips and confuse GetProjectDir().

Example: `ntm --robot-spawn=my--project --spawn-cc=2` would succeed in robot mode but fail in CLI.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run: `ntm --robot-spawn=my--project --spawn-cc=1 --dry-run` (no label)
2. Observe: No error — session 'my--project' is created despite containing '--'
3. Compare: `ntm spawn my--project --cc=1 --dry-run` correctly rejects with error
4. Also compare: `ntm add my--project --cc=1` (cli/add.go) — also allows '--' when no label

## Fix
Move ValidateProjectName() BEFORE the label check in robot/spawn.go, matching the CLI pattern.
Also apply the same fix to cli/add.go which has the same conditional pattern.

## Files to modify
- internal/robot/spawn.go: Move ValidateProjectName outside the if opts.Label != "" block
- internal/cli/add.go: Move ValidateProjectName outside the if label != "" block

## Testing
- Add test in robot/spawn_test.go: verify that SpawnSession with Session="my--project" and Label=""
  returns an error (currently it doesn't)
- Add test in cli/add_test.go or label_test.go: verify add rejects "--" names even without label

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] robot/spawn.go calls ValidateProjectName() unconditionally (before label check)
- [ ] cli/add.go calls ValidateProjectName() unconditionally (before label check)
- [ ] Test: robot spawn with Session='my--project' and Label='' returns error
- [ ] Test: cli add with project='my--project' and label='' returns error
- [ ] Existing tests pass: go test ./internal/config/ ./internal/cli/ ./internal/robot/
- [ ] Behavior is consistent across all entry points: spawn, create, quick, add, robot spawn","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:01.158656079Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:07.946239385Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2eblo","title":"Task: Implement conflict density metric","description":"# Task: Implement conflict density metric

Track disagreements per output pair.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/conflicts.go
type ConflictTracker struct {
    Conflicts []Conflict
}

type Conflict struct {
    Topic      string
    ModeA      string
    ModeB      string
    PositionA  string
    PositionB  string
    Severity   ConflictSeverity  // minor, moderate, major
    Resolved   bool
    Resolution string
}

type ConflictDensity struct {
    TotalConflicts      int
    ResolvedConflicts   int
    UnresolvedConflicts int
    ConflictsPerPair    float64
    HighConflictPairs   []string
    Source              string // "auditor" or "fallback"
}

func NewConflictTracker() *ConflictTracker
func (c *ConflictTracker) FromAudit(report *AuditReport) []Conflict
func (c *ConflictTracker) DetectConflicts(outputs []ModeOutput) []Conflict // fallback
func (c *ConflictTracker) GetDensity(totalPairs int) *ConflictDensity
func (c *ConflictTracker) GetHighConflictPairs(threshold int) []string
```

## Conflict Detection
- **Primary source:** DisagreementAuditor report (preferred)
- **Fallback:** heuristic from ModeOutput when auditor not run
  - contradictory theses (exact/normalized mismatch)
  - conflicting risk impact for same risk text
  - opposing recommendations on same topic

## Logging Requirements
- Log source (auditor vs fallback)
- Log counts + high-conflict pairs

## Tests
- Unit: density math for N conflicts across K pairs
- Unit: auditor → conflict conversion
- Unit: fallback detection on synthetic outputs

 {"id":"bd-2emf","title":"Feature: Resume session from checkpoint/handoff","description":"## Overview
Provide a **resume** flow that wraps existing checkpoint/handoff recovery so users can restore a session with assignments and context intact.

## Scope
- `ntm resume <session> [checkpoint-id]` as a thin wrapper over existing checkpoint/rollback + spawn recovery
- If no checkpoint provided: use most recent
- Include assignment summary + BV snapshot in the resume output
- Robot mode: structured output for success/failure (reuse RobotResponse)

## Non‑Goals
- New checkpoint storage format (extend existing)
- Automatic background recovery

## Tests
- Unit: checkpoint resolution logic (last/explicit)
- E2E: create checkpoint → kill session → resume → verify panes restored + summary output

## Logging
`log.Printf("[E2E-RESUME] session=%s checkpoint=%s restored=%t", …)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Resume works for recent checkpoints
- Clear errors when no checkpoint exists
- Robot output includes restore summary","notes":"Already implemented: resume.go exists in internal/cli/ with checkpoint/handoff recovery.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T08:41:17.651329886Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T03:53:22.348453596Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T03:53:22.348325327Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2emf","depends_on_id":"bd-32ck","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:14:41Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2emf","depends_on_id":"bd-3kthl","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:14:41Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2emf","depends_on_id":"bd-92e1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:14:41Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3no20","title":"LimitDetector: Pattern matching with configurable regex","description":"## LimitDetector: Pattern Matching with Configurable Regex

### Purpose
Implement pattern matching logic that detects usage limits in captured pane output.

### Implementation Details

**File:** Part of `internal/swarm/limit_detector.go`

**Pattern Matching:**
```go
// PatternMatcher checks output against limit patterns.
type PatternMatcher struct {
    // patterns maps agent type to compiled regexes
    patterns map[string][]*regexp.Regexp
}

// NewPatternMatcher creates matcher from config.
func NewPatternMatcher(limitPatterns map[string][]string) *PatternMatcher {
    pm := &PatternMatcher{
        patterns: make(map[string][]*regexp.Regexp),
    }
    
    for agentType, patternStrs := range limitPatterns {
        for _, p := range patternStrs {
            // Use case-insensitive matching
            re := regexp.MustCompile("(?i)" + regexp.QuoteMeta(p))
            pm.patterns[agentType] = append(pm.patterns[agentType], re)
        }
    }
    
    return pm
}

// Match checks if output matches any pattern for the agent type.
func (pm *PatternMatcher) Match(agentType, output string) (matched bool, pattern string) {
    patterns := pm.patterns[agentType]
    for _, re := range patterns {
        if re.MatchString(output) {
            return true, re.String()
        }
    }
    return false, ""
}
```

**Default Patterns:**
```go
var DefaultLimitPatterns = map[string][]string{
    "cc": {
        "You've hit your limit",
        "You have hit your limit",
        "usage limit",
        "Rate limit exceeded",
        "Please wait",
        "This conversation is getting long", // Context limit
    },
    "cod": {
        "You've hit your usage limit",
        "You have reached your usage limit",
        "Rate limit exceeded",
        "context limit reached",
    },
    "gmi": {
        "quota exceeded",
        "rate limit",
        "context window exceeded",
    },
}
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log pattern match: `[LimitDetector] pattern_matched agent_type=%s pattern=%q`
- Log no match: `[LimitDetector] no_match agent_type=%s` (debug level)

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Case-insensitive pattern matching
- [ ] Configurable patterns via config file
- [ ] Return matched pattern for logging
- [ ] Support both string literal and regex patterns","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:00:59.034358872Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:35:41.641621235Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:35:41.641559559Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3no20","depends_on_id":"bd-11y0m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:18:55.173804463Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3no20","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:19:18.404272120Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3nrl9","title":"Fix nil context panic in TestBuildMSComponent_MSNotInstalled","description":"## Context
Pre-existing test failure in internal/context/pack_test.go:785-794 that predates the --label
feature work. The test panics with "cannot create context from nil parent" because it passes
nil as the context to NewContextPackBuilder, which eventually reaches context.WithTimeout(nil, ...).

## Root cause
```go
func TestBuildMSComponent_MSNotInstalled(t *testing.T) {
    t.Parallel()
    b := NewContextPackBuilder(nil)  // <-- nil context
    comp := b.buildMSComponent(nil, "test query", 500)  // <-- nil context again
    ...
}
```
NewContextPackBuilder(nil) stores nil as the context. When buildMSComponent calls MSAdapter.runCommand(),
it calls context.WithTimeout(b.ctx, ...) where b.ctx is nil. Go's context.WithTimeout panics on nil parent.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run: go test ./internal/context/ -run TestBuildMSComponent_MSNotInstalled -v
2. Observe: panic: cannot create context from nil parent
3. Stack trace points to context.WithTimeout(b.ctx, ...) where b.ctx is nil

## Fix
Replace nil with context.Background() in the test:
```go
b := NewContextPackBuilder(context.Background())
comp := b.buildMSComponent(context.Background(), "test query", 500)
```

## Testing
- Run: go test ./internal/context/ -run TestBuildMSComponent -v
- Should either pass with "ms not installed" error or skip gracefully if ms is installed
- Run full test suite: go test ./... to verify no regressions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TestBuildMSComponent_MSNotInstalled passes without panic
- [ ] context.Background() used instead of nil in both constructor and method call
- [ ] No other tests in pack_test.go pass nil context (audit for similar issues)
- [ ] go test ./internal/context/ passes cleanly
- [ ] go test ./... shows no regressions","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:10.764291058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:22.438041506Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3nson","title":"Unit Tests: ntm assign command strategy selection and bead-agent matching","description":"## Test Coverage
Test the core ntm assign algorithm for strategy selection and bead-to-agent matching.

## Test Cases

### Strategy Selection
- Select balanced strategy by default
- Select speed strategy when --strategy=speed
- Select quality strategy when --strategy=quality
- Select dependency strategy when --strategy=dependency
- Select round-robin strategy when --strategy=round-robin
- Error on invalid strategy name

### Bead-to-Agent Matching
- Match beads to idle agents only
- Respect agent type filters (--cc-only, --cod-only, --gmi-only)
- Apply capability matrix for quality strategy
- Prioritize high unblock_count for dependency strategy
- Even distribution for round-robin strategy

### Edge Cases
- Zero beads available
- Zero idle agents
- More beads than agents
- More agents than beads
- Single agent, multiple beads
- Multiple agents, single bead

### Output Format
- JSON output includes assigned array
- JSON output includes skipped array with reasons
- Each assignment has pane, bead_id, agent_type

## Logging Requirements
- Log strategy selected
- Log agent pool before matching
- Log each matching decision with score/reason
- Log final assignment map

## Test File
